critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — SAFETY-NET DISTRIBUTION SHIFT: Unvalidated deployment extension in 6 months. No validation plan described.

C2 — CAPACITY-DRIVEN THRESHOLD: Threshold not validated against cost-benefit structure.

C3 — HRRP EXTERNALITY: HRRP-relevant diagnosis performance not evaluated.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — Concede as a gate condition for safety-net extension. Initial deployment to three existing hospitals is supported by validation data.

C2 — Concede. Cost-benefit analysis should be pre-launch requirement.

C3 — Concede. HRRP stratified analysis is feasible pre-launch using holdout data.
adjudication_raw: Defender concedes all three concerns as deployment governance requirements. Empirical test conditions: safety-net prospective validation, cost-benefit threshold analysis, HRRP-stratified performance check. All three are pre-deployment requirements.

empirical_test.measure: Prospective safety-net validation AUC-ROC; threshold break-even analysis using $45/$12,000 cost ratio; HRRP diagnosis-group recall in holdout
empirical_test.success_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC >= 0.68; threshold precision above break-even; HRRP groups recall comparable to overall model
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC substantially below existing hospitals; threshold below break-even; HRRP groups systematically missed
